config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja demi kepentingan masyarakat, karena tanpa perlindungan hak dasar, mayoritas bisa menindas kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak populer. Kepentingan bersama penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan menginjak kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum perorangan. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in my view that is exactly how abuse gets justified. From a Chinese perspective, I understand why people emphasize social stability and collective welfare, but I have also seen how easily “for the greater good” can be used to silence ordinary people and ignore their dignity. Society matters, but it should be built through fair rules that protect basic rights first, not by treating individual rights as disposable. If the rights of one person can be sacrificed too easily, then the society being protected is not truly healthy or just.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Indonesia kita sudah sering melihat bagaimana alasan “demi kepentingan umum” bisa dipakai untuk menekan yang lemah. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi kalau hak dasar tidak dilindungi, mayoritas atau negara bisa seenaknya menentukan siapa yang boleh dikorbankan. Buat saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling patuh pada kolektif, melainkan yang adil terhadap tiap orang, terutama saat ada kelompok kecil yang mudah diabaikan. Jadi, prioritasnya bukan masyarakat versus individu, melainkan bagaimana kepentingan bersama dicapai tanpa merampas martabat dan kebebasan perorangan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should have blanket priority over the rights of the individual, because in practice that often becomes a permission slip for power to ignore people. From my perspective in China, I understand why social order and collective goals are valued, but I also know that once individual rights are treated as secondary, the people with less power are the first to suffer. A society is only genuinely strong when it can pursue the common good without making basic dignity and legal protection negotiable. So I would put limits on the collective: society matters, but it should not be allowed to override fundamental individual rights whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus lebih diprioritaskan daripada hak individu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan. Bagi saya, justru ukuran masyarakat yang baik adalah apakah ia mampu melindungi orang yang lemah, minoritas, dan yang tidak punya kuasa, bukan seberapa patuh semua orang pada kolektif. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh dijadikan alasan untuk mengorbankan martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum dasar. Kalau hak individu bisa dikalahkan begitu saja, maka yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat sering kali hanya kepentingan pihak yang paling kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in real life that idea is too easy to turn into an excuse for abuse. From my perspective in China, social harmony and collective welfare are important, but I have seen how quickly “for the common good” can become a way to silence ordinary people and ignore fairness. A society is not truly strong if it asks the weak to give up their basic dignity whenever it is convenient for those in power. The common good should be pursued through rules that protect individual rights first, not by treating those rights as optional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
